known_unknowns.question: How should AVC apply to the 10 multi-fund 'common cost' WBSs (each with >10 distinct funds posting in 2024-2026)? WBSs identified: PR00027627 (1,211 funds, Management Cost PFF technical account), PR00000106 (592, Major Programme 1 Education), PR00000107 (591, Major Programme 2 Natural Sciences), PR00000111 (448, RP Cross-Cutting Themes), PR00000109 (369, Major Programme 4 Culture), PR00000108 (275, Major Programme 3 Soc & Hum Sciences), PR00000110 (249, Major Programme 5 Com & Information), PR00008981 (68, UBO FITOCA), PR00002724 (36, IESALC), PR00008982 (30, UBO FITOCA PSC EAR). These cannot be evaluated with a single (Fund, WBS) pool view because the AVC concept does not map cleanly to common-cost / overhead objects.
known_unknowns.why: The Pool Health Matrix (run 2026-05-01) flagged these 10 WBSs as outliers: 3,455 pair-rows excluded from single-pool eval. Without a Budget Office decision, these objects sit in a governance limbo: FM-AVC sees a fund-level bucket, PS-AVC sees a single WBS pool, and neither view aligns with operational reality (the WBS hosts costs from hundreds of funds). Proportional allocation (as used for DERIVED-MULTI 2-10 fund cases) is mathematically possible but administratively unreasonable for 1,211 funds.
known_unknowns.how_to_resolve: Submit list to HQ Budget Office with options: (a) PROPORTIONAL — extend matrix's DERIVED-MULTI logic with no upper limit, accepting the administrative cost; (b) PER-BUCKET — switch AVC at the (Fund, FundCenter, CommItem) granularity for these WBSs and ignore PS-pool; (c) AVC-OFF — exempt these WBSs from PS-AVC entirely, rely solely on FM-AVC at their fund level; (d) RE-DESIGN — split the WBSs into per-programme child WBSs (governance-heavy).

known_unknowns.question: Why does UNESCO not run a symmetric biennium-end protocol mirroring FM revenue transfer with PS budget transfer (KBW1 cross-project), WBS TECO/CLSD on legacy, PO reassignment ME22N, and Workplan update? Are the missing 4 actions intentional policy or undocumented manual practice?
known_unknowns.how_to_resolve: Interview HQ Budget Office on biennium-end SOP. Compare with prior biennium close 196EAR4040.
known_unknowns.answer: UNESCO does NOT have a formal protocol for biennium-end closeout. Confirmed by user (Session #69, 2026-05-01). No written SOP, no cross-biennium WBS-mapping table, no documented end-to-end owner. The biennium-end is operated as 5 independent ad-hoc actions by separate teams (Finance does FB01 R1 batch; Programme does Workplan reprogramming; PS team does TECO/CLSD when manually triggered; MM team does ME22N PO reassignment if requested; nobody synchronizes the 5).
known_unknowns.implication: This eliminates Plan A (formalize existing SOP). The gap is BOTH organizational (no SOP exists) AND technical (no enforcement). FIX-B requires building a SOP from scratch, FIX-C requires creating master-data mapping that does not exist. Therefore: FIX-E (HARD-BLOCK BAdI) and FIX-F (Meta-AVC) become the load-bearing solutions because they enforce consistency without requiring the missing organizational layer.

known_unknowns.question: Where exactly in the MULESOFT integration does the FIPEX-aware Commitment_Item__c granularity from Allocation__c get flattened to FIPEX=REVENUE in SAP FMIFIIT?
known_unknowns.how_to_resolve: Find MULESOFT flow that calls BAPI_FUND_CREATE/FB01 for fund revenue. Inspect field mapping for Commitment_Item__c.
known_unknowns.partial_answer: Explore agent search 2026-05-02 found smoking-gun ABAP code in extracted_code: YCL_YPS8_BCS_BL===CM005_RPY.abap lines 58 and 73 explicitly preserve FIPEX=REVENUE rows while deleting zero-amount operational FIPEX rows: DELETE LT_WFMBDT WHERE HAMOUNT IS INITIAL AND FIPEX <> 'REVENUE'. This is a UNESCO custom PS budget class (BCS = Budget Control System) — confirms REVENUE placeholder is hardcoded in UNESCO custom code. However, the MULESOFT flow itself (MUNESCO_FundCreate or equivalent) is NOT in our extracted code repos — it lives in MuleSoft GitLab/Artifactory. Mapping table Allocation__c.Commitment_Item__c -> FMBL.CMMTITEM is DOCUMENTED as synced in unescrp/.claude/skills/salesforce-integration/session-learnings.md but empirical fmifiit_full shows NO per-FIPEX distribution at fund creation — gap between documented mapping and runtime behavior.
known_unknowns.next_action: Contact MuleSoft Ops Team to obtain the MUNESCO_FundCreate flow XML from their repository. In parallel, contact FM Master Data Functional Lead (possibly R. RIOS based on session #66 context) to identify any Z* BAPI wrapper that intercepts BAPI_FUND_CREATE on the SAP side.
